cases.message: I've been having crushing chest pain for the past 20 minutes that radiates to my left arm. I'm sweating a lot and feel nauseous. My grandfather died of a heart attack and this feels exactly like what he described.
I woke up with the worst headache of my life. It came on suddenly, like a thunderclap. My neck feels stiff and I'm sensitive to light. I've never had a headache like this before.
My face feels droopy on the left side. My left arm is very weak and I'm having trouble speaking clearly. It started about 15 minutes ago out of nowhere.
I'm having severe difficulty breathing. My lips feel like they're turning blue and I can't catch my breath even sitting still. I used my inhaler twice but it's not helping.
I was stung by a bee 10 minutes ago and my throat is starting to swell. I have a known bee allergy. My lips feel tingly and I feel dizzy. I don't have my EpiPen with me.
My husband is diabetic and I found him confused and shaking in the kitchen. He's wearing a medical alert bracelet. He doesn't recognise me and I can't get him to drink juice.

I have sudden tearing pain that started in my chest and is moving to my back between my shoulder blades. It came on instantly and is the worst pain I've ever had. I have a history of high blood pressure.
I was in a car accident 20 minutes ago. I hit my head on the steering wheel. I'm conscious but I have a deep cut on my forehead that won't stop bleeding and my neck really hurts.
I suddenly can't breathe and my right leg has been swollen and painful for 3 days after a long flight. I feel like my heart is racing and I'm lightheaded.
My 2-year-old has a purple non-blanching rash spreading across her torso and she has a high fever of 40°C. She's lethargic and won't wake up properly. She had a mild cold yesterday.
I took too many of my sleeping tablets about 30 minutes ago. I'm feeling very drowsy now. I'm not sure exactly how many I took but the bottle is nearly empty.
I'm 32 weeks pregnant and I've just had a seizure. My partner is calling for me. I have pre-eclampsia and my blood pressure has been very high this week.
I have sudden severe pain in my right testicle. It came on out of nowhere 30 minutes ago. It's extremely tender and the testicle looks higher than normal. I'm 17 years old.
My whole family — me, my wife, and two kids — all have severe headaches and feel dizzy. We've been indoors all day with the heating on. The dog also seems unwell.
I'm 8 weeks pregnant and I have severe sharp pain on my lower right side. I'm feeling faint and there's some vaginal bleeding. The pain came on suddenly about an hour ago.
My father has been having a seizure continuously for over 5 minutes and it hasn't stopped. He has epilepsy but his seizures never last this long. He's not coming round.
I have a terrible headache, my vision is blurry, and I'm seeing halos around lights. My eye is extremely painful and red. I feel like I'm going to vomit. I'm 65 years old.
I'm burning from a chemical splash at work. The chemical got into my eyes and I've been rinsing them for 5 minutes but they're still burning intensely. I can barely keep them open.
I have a fever of 39.8°C, I'm confused, my blood pressure is very low according to the machine at the pharmacy, and I have a wound on my leg from surgery last week that looks infected.
My 4-year-old is drooling and can't swallow. She's making a high-pitched noise when she breathes in and she's sitting forward with her neck stretched out. She had a mild fever this morning.
I have sudden severe pain in my right eye and I can see a shadow or curtain coming in from the right side. I'm also seeing flashes of light. It happened instantly 45 minutes ago.
I just ran a marathon and my urine is dark brown, almost like cola. I have severe muscle cramps in both legs and I feel extremely weak. I've never had this happen after a race before.
I have crushing chest pain that gets better when I lean forward. I had a respiratory illness last week and now the chest pain is back. It's sharp and worse when I breathe deeply.
I'm 22 years old and I get chest pain and feel like I'm going to faint when I play football. It's happened three times now. Today I actually fainted briefly on the pitch.

My mother swallowed a large fish bone at dinner 2 hours ago. She can feel it stuck in her throat, she's having trouble swallowing even saliva, and she's drooling. She can still breathe but it's uncomfortable.
I've had a fever of 39.5°C for the past 3 days. I also have a sharp pain in my right ear and the pain is getting worse. Paracetamol isn't bringing the fever down much.
I've had a persistent cough for about 2 weeks now. It started as a dry cough but now I'm coughing up some yellowish-green mucus. I've also had a low-grade fever and feel tired.
My vision in my right eye suddenly got blurry about an hour ago. It's like a curtain came down over the bottom half. There's no pain but it hasn't gone away.
I've had a pain in my lower right abdomen for about 12 hours. It started around my belly button and has moved to the right side. It gets worse when I walk. I feel sick to my stomach.
I've been having a burning sensation when I urinate for the past 2 days. I need to go very frequently but only pass a small amount. My lower abdomen is tender and my urine smells different.
My left calf has been swollen, red, and warm to the touch since yesterday. It's quite painful when I press it or when I walk. I was on a long-haul flight 4 days ago.
I have a painful blistering rash along the left side of my torso. It wraps around from my back to my front. It started 3 days ago as a burning sensation and now the blisters have appeared.
I slipped on ice and fell onto my outstretched hand. My wrist is very swollen, painful to move, and there's some bruising. I can move my fingers but rotating my wrist is agony.
I have a severely sore throat — so bad I can barely swallow water. There are white patches on my tonsils. I've had a fever of 38.8°C for 2 days and my neck glands are swollen.
I have severe pain in my right flank that comes in waves. It started about 3 hours ago. I noticed my urine has a pink tinge to it. I feel nauseous and I've vomited twice.
I've been having palpitations — my heart feels like it's fluttering irregularly. It's been on and off for 2 days. I checked my pulse and it feels irregular. I'm 58 with no heart history.
I've had back pain for a week but this morning I woke up and my right leg feels weak and I have pins and needles from my lower back all the way down to my foot. I'm struggling to lift my foot.
I noticed a new lump in my left breast about a week ago. It's about the size of a marble, feels hard and doesn't move much. There's no pain. I'm 42 and had a normal mammogram 18 months ago.
I've lost about 7 kg in the last 2 months without trying to. I haven't changed my diet or exercise. I've been sweating a lot at night and feeling more tired than usual.
I've noticed fresh red blood in my stool twice in the last week. I'm 50 years old and I've never had this before. I don't have haemorrhoids as far as I know. There's no pain.

I feel exhausted all the time. It's been going on for about a month now. I sleep 8 hours but still wake up tired. I've also been gaining weight and feeling cold all the time. No other obvious symptoms.
I had an episode 2 hours ago where my left arm went weak and my speech was slurred for about 10 minutes. Then it completely resolved. I feel completely normal now but it scared me.
The wound on my forearm from a cut 3 days ago has red streaks spreading up my arm from it. It's more swollen than yesterday and feels warm. I have a mild fever of 38°C.
I woke up this morning and can't hear anything in my left ear. There was no warning — I just went to bed fine and woke up completely deaf in that ear. No pain, no infection.
I'm 8 weeks pregnant and I've been spotting light pink blood for 2 days. I have some mild cramping on and off. I had a miscarriage last year and I'm very worried.
My eyes and skin have turned slightly yellow over the past 3 days. My urine is dark like tea and my stools are pale. I feel generally unwell with some abdominal discomfort on the right side.
I have a painful red swollen joint in my big toe. It came on overnight, I didn't injure it. The skin over it is shiny and it's extremely tender — even the bed sheet touching it is painful.
My child has had a fever of 39°C for 2 days, a widespread pink rash, red cracked lips, and her eyes look red. She's been very irritable. She's 3 years old.
I've had a mole on my back for years but my partner noticed it's changed — it now has an irregular edge, has got bigger, and there's a dark spot in it that wasn't there before.
I have persistent heartburn that I've been treating with over-the-counter antacids for 3 months, but it's getting worse not better. I sometimes feel like food gets stuck when I swallow.
I have a very painful, swollen tooth and my face is puffy on the right side. I have a high fever of 38.9°C and the swelling seems to be spreading toward my jaw. I can't fully open my mouth.
My 10-month-old baby has been vomiting and screaming with severe abdominal pain in waves. Between the episodes she goes limp and pale. Her nappies have had some jelly-like red substance in them.
I have painful swollen lymph nodes in my neck and armpits for the past 3 weeks. I've been drenching my bed with sweat at night and I've lost some weight without trying.
I've been getting chest pain and shortness of breath when I climb stairs over the past month. It fully goes away when I rest. I'm 61 years old, I smoke, and I have diabetes.

My blood pressure has been very high when I check it at home — around 175/110 for the past week. I've been getting headaches and some visual disturbances. I'm on BP medication but it doesn't seem to be working.
My 6-year-old has a high-pitched barking cough and is making a noise when she breathes in, especially at night. She has a low fever. She had a cold last week.
I have a severe first-time migraine with flashing zigzag lights in my vision before the headache started. The headache is on one side and I feel very nauseous. I've never had this before.
I fell and hit my head an hour ago. I was briefly confused but feel better now. I have a headache and feel slightly nauseous. I didn't lose consciousness. Should I be seen?
I have numbness and tingling in both my hands, especially at night. I've been waking up with numb hands for a few months. My work involves a lot of typing. The right hand is worse.
I have really bad pain when I urinate, a discharge from my penis, and I've had unprotected sex with a new partner 5 days ago. I'm embarrassed but I think I need to be tested.
I have a runny nose, mild sore throat, and I've been sneezing a lot since yesterday. No fever. I feel a bit tired but otherwise okay. I think I caught a cold from my colleague.
I've had a mild headache for the past few hours. I've been staring at my computer screen all day for a long meeting. I haven't had much water today either.
I cut my finger while chopping vegetables about 20 minutes ago. The bleeding has stopped with pressure. The cut is maybe 1 cm long and not very deep. It stings a bit.
I spent too long at the beach today and I've got a mild sunburn on my shoulders and arms. The skin is red and slightly warm. I don't have any blistering.
I twisted my ankle during a run this morning. It's a bit swollen and sore to walk on but I can put weight on it. I've iced it and it feels a bit better. This has happened before.
I was bitten by a mosquito last night and the bite is red, swollen, and itchy. The area is about 3 cm across. I've had this kind of reaction before. No spreading redness or fever.
I have loose stools and mild stomach cramps since this morning. I think it might be something I ate at a restaurant last night. I've been to the toilet 4 times. No blood, no fever.
I have my usual period cramps today. They're the same as every month — uncomfortable but manageable with ibuprofen. No heavier bleeding than usual, no new symptoms.
I've got a sore lower back after spending the weekend gardening. It came on yesterday. I can move around but it aches. I've had this kind of back pain before and it usually goes in a few days.
I've got a cold sore on my lip. It started as a tingle yesterday and now there's a small blister. I get these a few times a year. I have cream at home that I use for them.
I have a mild burning sensation in my chest after eating a big dinner with wine. It's classic heartburn. I get this occasionally and it usually settles with antacids.
My eyes are itchy, watery, and slightly swollen. My nose is runny too. It's the same every year around this time — hay fever season. I've run out of my antihistamines.
I went to the gym for the first time in months yesterday and now my legs, arms, and back are very sore. Moving hurts but it's clearly just muscle ache. No swelling or injury.
I have a mild toothache when I eat or drink something cold. It's been going on for a week. No swelling, no fever, just sensitivity to cold. I think I might have a sensitive tooth.
I have a patch of dry, itchy skin on my elbow. I have eczema and this happens from time to time. The skin is red and flaky but not cracked or infected.
My toddler has a runny nose, mild cough, and a temperature of 37.8°C. She's eating less but still drinking. She's grumpy but responsive. She had her last vaccines 2 months ago.
I woke up with a stiff neck this morning. I must have slept in a bad position. I can move it slowly but turning quickly is painful. No tingling down my arms.
I banged my shin on a coffee table this morning. It's bruised and a bit swollen but I can walk normally. The skin isn't broken. Ice seems to be helping.
I've been feeling bloated and gassy after eating a big bean and lentil dish last night. I have mild stomach cramps but no diarrhea. I know these foods affect me like this.
I've had hiccups for about 30 minutes. They started during dinner. They're a bit uncomfortable but not distressing. I've tried holding my breath but they keep coming back.
I have a small paper cut on my thumb from opening an envelope. It stings when I bend it. There's a tiny bit of bleeding. I just want to know if I need to do anything special for it.
My eyes feel tired and dry after a long day reading. I work in a very air-conditioned office. I use reading glasses and I've been on my phone all evening too.
I have a slightly sore throat that started this morning. No fever, no difficulty swallowing, no white patches. I've been speaking a lot at work this week and the air conditioning is very dry.
I feel a bit nauseous and have a mild headache after a night out. I had quite a lot to drink last night. I've been drinking water and I feel a bit better than this morning.
My shoulders and upper back feel tense and achy. I've been very stressed at work this week and I've been hunching over my laptop. It feels like muscular tension rather than anything else.
I have chest tightness and my heart is racing. I have a big presentation in an hour and I'm really anxious. This has happened before big events for me. I feel a bit short of breath too.
I've had a stomach ache for the past hour after eating a really spicy curry. I feel bloated and gassy. I've had this before with spicy food and it usually goes away after a few hours.

I noticed my hands have been trembling slightly for the past two weeks — especially when I'm trying to hold something still, not when I'm moving. I've also had some trouble swallowing solid food occasionally. I'm 58 years old.
My 80-year-old mother has become more confused than usual over the past 2 days. She's been falling asleep in her chair a lot. She had a mild urine infection last month. She doesn't have dementia.
My 7-year-old has had a fever for 5 days, joint pain in his knees and ankles, and a salmon-coloured rash that comes and goes. He seems very unwell. He had a throat infection 3 weeks ago.
I've been gaining weight, feeling cold all the time, losing hair, and feeling completely exhausted for the past 3 months. My skin is very dry and I feel low in mood. I'm 34 and female.
I've been getting recurring headaches with zigzag visual disturbances for the past month. They last about 20 minutes and then a moderate headache follows. I've had 5 episodes now.
I've been having hiccups continuously for 2 days now. They won't stop no matter what I try. I haven't changed my diet. I have a mild headache as well. I'm 45 and otherwise healthy.
I had my first ever panic attack 30 minutes ago. My heart was racing, I was sweating, I couldn't breathe, and I thought I was dying. I'm calm now but shaken. I'm 28 and have no heart history.
I have pins and needles and numbness in both my feet and lower legs. It's been gradually getting worse over 3 months. I'm 52 and I have type 2 diabetes that isn't very well controlled.
My 2-year-old swallowed a small coin about an hour ago. She's not choking and seems fine. She's breathing normally and isn't distressed. I'm not sure if I need to do anything.
My 78-year-old grandmother fell getting out of the bath this morning. She's complaining of hip pain and can't put weight on her right leg. She says she didn't hit her head. She has osteoporosis.
I've been getting headaches almost every day for the past 2 months. They're moderate, mostly at the front of my head, and worse in the afternoon. I take paracetamol almost daily for them.
I'm 19 weeks pregnant and I've been getting mild cramps in my lower abdomen. They come and go and feel like mild period pains. I haven't had any bleeding. My last scan was normal at 12 weeks.

I feel generally unwell — tired, low-grade fever of 37.8°C, mild sore throat, and slightly swollen glands in my neck. I'm 19 years old. My flatmate had similar symptoms last week and was off for 10 days.
